content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Bill Kampe 
Mayor, City of Pacific Grove 
832 Lighthouse A venue 
Pacific Grove, CA 93950 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-15-047 
Dear Mr. Kampe: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
April 7, 2015 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the ••Act").1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest 
prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
May you participate in the City's consideration of an admission tax that may apply to 
movie theaters, musical performances, athletic events and the Monterey Bay Aquarium, despite 
your spouse selling greeting cards to a company that operates the Aquarium gift shop? 
CONCLUSION 
You may participate in the City's consideration of an admission tax since it would not be 
applied on your spouse's business or her source of income. 
FACTS 
The City of Pacific Grove is considering an admission tax that may apply to movie 
theaters, musical performances, and athletic events. The tax may also apply to admission to the 
Monterey Bay Aquarium, which is the largest venue to which the tax may apply. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-15-047 
Page No. 2 
Your spouse sells greeting cards to Service Solutions Associates (SSA) which operates 
the gift store at the Aquarium, as well as 30 other venues. You stated that SSA makes ordering 
decision, within guidelines set by the Aquarium. Sales to SSA have been slightly more than 
$2,000 per year. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
using his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a 
financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest". in a governmental decision, within 
the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect on one or more of the official's interests. (Section 87103.) 
You have identified two interest recognized in Section 87103. 
• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more. 
It appears that your spouse has a business making greeting cards which size sells to SSA. 
• Any source of income aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, 
received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is 
made. 
SSA would be considered a source of income to you by virtue of your community property 
interest in your spouse's income. It does 1101 appear that the Aquarium itself is an interest 
of yours as described in Section 87103. 
Foreseeability a11d Materiality: 
Neither SSA nor your spouse's business are explicitly involved in the city council 
decision on the admission tax. Thus, the financial effect is foreseeable if it is a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. If the financial result cannot be expected 
absent extraordinary circumstances not subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably 
foreseeable. 
The admission tax will not be imposed on SSA or your spouse's business. These entities 
will only be affected if the fee imposed on the Aquarium is somehow passed onto these other 
businesses by the Aquarium. There are no facts to suggest that the Aquarium will pass these 
expenses to SSA or your spouse's business. It is far more likely that the Aquarium will absorb 
the cost or pass it on to visitors. 


File No. A-15-047 
Page No. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Hyla Wagner 
General Counsel 
allace 
General Counsel

CONCLUSION: You may participate in the City's consideration of an admission tax since it would not be
